author: EmanueleCoradin
body:
This PR results from a work in collaboration with @valsdav. Thanks also to @Electricks94 for the hints resulted in the float16_t type.

## PR description:

This PR introduces FP16 (half precision) inference support in the PyTorch Alpaka pipeline, alongside the existing FP32 execution path.
FP16 results are expected to be close to FP32, but not identical due to reduced precision.

Two FP16 workflows are now supported:

### Runtime FP16 conversion
Input data is stored in SoA format with full FP32 precision.
Conversion to FP16 happens at inference time via the PyTorch API, enabled by passing torch::kHalf to model.forward(...).
### Native FP16 pipeline
Input features are stored directly in FP16 SoA format using cms::float16_t.
Data is consumed end-to-end as half precision tensors.
If the model is loaded in FP16 precision, no runtime conversion is required.

## PR Validation
A small inspection module has been extended to compare FP32, runtime FP16, and native FP16 outputs.
The module checks that the relative difference between FP32 and FP16 output is less then 1%.

They can be run by:

```sh
cd PhysicsTools/PyTorchAlpakaTest/test
cmsRun runPyTorchAlpakaTest.py -e 3
```
title: Add Torch FP16 inference support
